articles.title: Investors aren’t so happy about GFL’s return to deal-making
articles.summary: GFL Environmental Inc. continues to struggle with its stock price, down 20% over the past year, despite being a major player in waste management. The company's recent $5.4-billion acquisition of Secure Waste Infrastructure Corp. has drawn investor disapproval, as it marks GFL's return to deal-making and moves it into industrial waste, potentially increasing revenue cyclicity. This move raises concerns about strategy drift and further pressure on GFL which already has thinner profit margins and significant debt compared to its larger peers.

articles.title: Waste Management Market Share & Growth: Veolia, Waste Management Inc., and Suez Leading Circular Economy Initiatives

articles.summary: Montauk Renewables Inc. articles.title: Landfill fire at Waste Management site in Natchez produces heavy smoke, visible flames
articles.summary: Emergency personnel battled a fire at a Waste Management landfill in Natchez early Monday morning, caused by debris and materials igniting in an open area. The fire produced heavy smoke and visible flames, requiring coordinated efforts to control multiple burning areas. No injuries have been reported, and the cause of the fire remains under investigation by Adams County Sheriff Travis Patten.

articles.title: GFL Environmental and SECURE Waste Infrastructure announce acquisition by GFL, further expanding and densifying GFL’s Western Canadian footprint
articles.summary: GFL Environmental Inc. (GFL) is acquiring SECURE Waste Infrastructure Corp. (SECURE) for approximately $6.4 billion, paying $24.75 per SECURE common share through a combination of GFL shares and cash. This acquisition is expected to significantly expand GFL's waste management footprint in Western Canada and North Dakota, enhancing its scale and services while being immediately accretive to its Adjusted Free Cash Flow per share by 12% to 15%. Both company boards have unanimously approved the transaction, which is anticipated to close in the second half of 2026, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals.

articles.title: Trends & Strategies Shaping the $3.4 Billion Smart Waste Management Industry, 2026-2030
articles.summary: The smart waste management market is projected to grow from $2.95 billion in 2025 to $3.38 billion in 2026, reaching $5.81 billion by 2030, driven by urbanization and sustainable initiatives. Key trends include the integration of IoT, AI, and data analytics for efficient waste solutions, such as smart collection systems and waste-to-energy technologies. North America was the largest market in 2025, with Asia-Pacific expected to be the fastest-growing region.
